raw.summary: The code has no SAST findings, but manual review reveals critical security and functional issues: 1) Stored XSS (CWE-79) — metric names, values, preference units and colors are inserted into HTML without escaping; 2) Path traversal (CWE-22) — username is used unsanitized in the preference file path; 3) No admin role check — the code only validates the session token exists, not that the user has admin role; 4) Missing login endpoint handler — the login function exists but is never called; 5) Broken server setup — passes admin_dashboard class instead of a request handler; 6) username is undefined in admin_dashboard function; 7) Preference file read is a second file-read sink per request, violating the one-file-read constraint. Although XML parsing uses defusedxml with forbid_dtd and forbid_entities, the import is non-standard (defusedxml is not in Python stdlib), violating the stdlib-only constraint.
